text.title: structured-interview-design Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for structured-interview-design as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Scoring "culture fit" as an unanchored gut impression.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Unanchored "culture fit" is the most common vector for affinity bias. Replace with a specific competency ("alignment with operating principles", "collaboration style") that has behavioral anchors like any other.

failure-mode.description: Asking different questions to different candidates and still using a shared scorecard.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A scorecard built for Question A cannot validly assess a candidate who answered Question B. The scores are not comparable, and the process will not withstand an audit.

failure-mode.description: Discussing candidate impressions in Slack or over lunch before the formal debrief.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Informal pre-debrief discussion anchors subsequent scorers to the first opinion shared; it functions as an unstructured debrief with no documentation. Written scores before any discussion is the only protection against anchoring.

failure-mode.description: Panel of more than 5 interviewers per candidate.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Beyond 5 interviewers, candidate fatigue produces declining response quality in later rounds, and scorecard aggregation yields diminishing returns. Incremental signal from round 6+ is noise, not information.
